title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Scope Transfer
summary: A team says “build a maze solver” without defining the player task, puzzle rules, assistance, supported input, repository state, or proof of success. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the scope transfer increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.
Separate pure-model, algorithm, Tcl interpreter, Tk widget, display, input, accessibility, package, and player evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 4460 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; the prior good state must survive failure; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer. A defensible solution applies Scope Transfer, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-scope-transfer — State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_outcomes_repo_accessibility_2(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python maze decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected grid, topology, frontier, path, callback, state, accessibility, save, package, or recovery case, then state which Tcl, Tk, tkinter, Canvas, display, focus, input, assistive-technology, filesystem, timing, installer, or production claim still needs controlled native transfer evidence.

Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-1lc0jx
# Competency: maze-scope-transfer.
# Case 9633: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_scope_transfer_1lc0jx(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lc0jx = "1lc0jx-xj0cl1-9633"
    assert evidence_variant_1lc0jx
    evidence_axes_1lc0jx = (
        "grid1lc0jx",
        "topology9633",
        "searchxj0cl1",
        "event1lc0jx9633",
        "access96331lc0jx",
        "recoveryxj0cl19633",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lc0jx)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

steps.why: Retrieving Scope Transfer through the concrete player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Scope Transfer model · Theory Scope Transfer, keep Scope Transfer connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Scope Transfer model · Theory Scope Transfer, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Scope Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Scope Transfer model · Theory Scope Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2650 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: State which claims browser models can establish and which require a controlled native desktop transfer.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Scope Transfer model · Theory Scope Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Pyodide behavior proves Tkinter behavior on every desktop.
